exemplar.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Clear, sustained focus on the prescribed title.
Effective, discipline-specific examples in both mathematics (exponential growth, NavierâStokes) and natural sciences (ideal gas law, Newtonâs laws).
Coherent argumentation with awareness of alternative viewpoints and tentative evaluation of simplicity versus accuracy versus utility.
weaknesses: Evaluation of counterclaims remains surface-level, with limited critical weighing of implications.
Occasional structural repetition and lack of synthesis across the two areas of knowledge.
Key concepts (âutility,â âaccuracy,â âmodelâ) are asserted rather than rigorously analysed, and formal expressions of important models are omitted.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The overall band-7 evaluation reflects a well-focused essay that links arguments to the title and areas of knowledge with relevant examples, demonstrating coherent reasoning and some evaluation of alternative perspectives. However, the critical engagement is not consistently in-depth: counterclaims are stated rather than fully interrogated, key terms are under-defined, and the structure leads to repetition rather than integrated synthesis. These characteristics align clearly with the lower end of the 7â8 band, justifying a final mark of 7 out of 10.

annotations.comment: Reference to Van der Waals is superficial. Compare the magnitude of deviations between models to show quantitatively when a simpler model loses utility.

The stated knowledge question (âTo what extent does simplicity enhance the utility of knowledgeâ) diverges from the prescribed titleâs focus on wrongness and usefulness. Revise the knowledge question to align precisely with Boxâs aphorism.
This sentence fragment ends abruptly (âthey are neverthelessâ). Complete the thought to clarify the point about the utility of imperfect mathematical models.
Key terms like âutilityâ and âaccuracyâ are asserted without unpacking. Define these terms explicitly and discuss their implications for knowledge production.

The essayâs framing of the prescribed title drifts toward a discussion of simplicity rather than directly engaging Boxâs claim. The student should explicitly link each analysis back to the exact wording âall models are wrong, but some are useful.â

Early definitions of âmodelâ in mathematics versus natural sciences remain broad and declarative. The student should refine these definitions, showing how they relate specifically to knowledge production and Boxâs assertion.

The concluding section summarises previous points but does not deepen reflection on how this affects knowledge production. Strengthen the conclusion by evaluating implications for theory and application.
Several ideas are restated verbatim from earlier paragraphs. Rather than repetition, aim for synthesis that connects insights from both areas of knowledge more cohesively.
The exponential growth model is described verbally but lacks its formal expression (e.g. $$y=a e^{bt}$$). Including the equation would strengthen the mathematical rigour of the analysis.
The counterclaim that abstraction improves utility is noted but not critically weighed. Explore how ignoring external factors affects both strengths and limitations in practice.
The in-text reference to â1.03 Applicationsâ is unclear and inconsistent with IB citation standards. Adopt a consistent academic referencing style for all sources.
The term ânavier stokesâ should be capitalized and spelled correctly as âNavierâStokes.â Consistent technical naming is essential for clarity.
The discussion of complex but âaccurateâ models versus utility feels rushed. Provide specific examples of where NavierâStokes becomes impractical to solidify this contrast.

The NavierâStokes equations are described in words but the student does not present their form or discuss the unsolved existence and smoothness problem, which would deepen the evaluation of their utility versus theoretical value.
Mentioning chaos theory opens a new thread but lacks clear connection to Boxâs claim. Explain how sensitivity to initial conditions affects a modelâs practical usefulness.
The paragraph focuses sequentially on mathematics without synthesizing insights across AOKs. Consider comparative analysis rather than treating each area in isolation.
This sentence ends mid-thought (âthis model is alsoâ). Complete the explanation of the ideal gas model to maintain coherence.
Several sentences use passive constructions (âis deemed,â âis consideredâ). Rewriting in active voice would improve clarity and reader engagement.
The introduction to natural sciences remains abstract. Include concrete experimental examples or data to illustrate how models in this AOK are âwrong but useful.â
The discussion of the ideal gas model would benefit from presenting its law $$PV=nRT$$. Including the equation would ground the argument in the formalism of natural sciences.
When discussing Newtonâs laws, include the formula $$F=ma$$ and specify the domain of validity. This strengthens the precision of the natural sciences analysis.
The implications of highly accurate models for the production of knowledge are mentioned but not explored. Discuss how precision influences theoretical versus practical knowledge claims.
The final body paragraph merges into the conclusion without clear structural separation. Consider signposting or separate headings for conclusion to improve clarity.
Final terms such as âabstractionâ and âgeneralisationâ are used broadly. Define them explicitly in the context of model-based knowledge to avoid ambiguity.
Several bibliographic entries omit publication dates or use informal URLs. Ensure all references follow a consistent academic style with full details.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
